title: Self-Guided Berlin Walking Tour with Audio - Quick Summary
items: **Choose the route by its real start and finish, not only by the topic.** Nordbahnhof, Anhalter Bahnhof, the World Clock, the Reichstag and East Side Gallery sit in different parts of Berlin.
**A useful audio walk is more than an MP3.** It needs a named starting point, short place-linked chapters, written directions or map links, an honest time range and an offline backup.
**The five paid routes cost €4.99 each.** Walking times range from 75 to 120 minutes: Medieval Berlin is shortest, while Hidden Berlin, Third Reich Berlin and Kreuzberg can take closer to two hours.
**Add 30 minutes before any hard deadline.** Exhibitions, photographs, memorial panels, construction and station exits can turn a 90-minute route into a longer walk.
**Use a live guide for first-day orientation and questions.** Use audio later for one focused subject at your own pace; the free tour does not follow the full Berlin Wall route.
**Prepare the evening before.** Save the start, finish and return connection, download the audio and route card on Wi-Fi, charge the phone and keep one ear free at crossings.
